spec_match.rationale: The lab now checks Request.prototype, shows that page code cannot construct a true navigate-mode Request, and models the service-worker FetchEvent values for reload and history cases.

interactive.rationale: The page has live Request probes plus a scenario selector that updates native/fallback reload decisions and cache strategy output.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The compatibility lab still compares native support against fallback heuristics and now more accurately explains the constructor and service-worker boundary.

edge_cases.rationale: It covers unsupported attributes, rejected navigate-mode construction, history restores, form POST navigations, absent fallback signals, and ambiguous fallback heuristics.

references.rationale: The concept includes the required https://chromestatus.com/feature/5154214529597440 link plus Fetch and MDN references.

design_system.rationale: Raw badge colors were replaced with shared variables, and the local button background override now also sets color.

summary: The missing ChromeStatus reference and design-token issues are fixed, and the lab now corrects the Request constructor boundary while keeping the interactive fallback comparison.
